name: Blackberry, Apple, Orange and Juniper Butter
recipeInstructions:
1. Roughly chop the apples, including the peel and core. Place in a preserving pan with the blackberries. Peel the skin from the oranges and discard; chop the flesh. Squeeze the juice from the lemons. Add the orange flesh, lemon juice and skins to the pan. Pour over the cranberry juice and add the juniper berries; bring gently to the boil. Continue to boil quite hard for 25 minutes until the fruit is soft and pulpy.
2. Remove the lemon skins and leave to cool. Squeeze the lemon pulp into the mixture and discard the skins. Push all the pulp through a fine nylon sieve and weigh; weigh out three-quarters of this measurement in sugar.
3. Place the pulp and sugar in a pan and dissolve the sugar over a low heat. Increase the heat and bring to a boil. Simmer for about 15 minutes until set, stirring often and mixing in any scum present as this is where the pectin is located. When setting point is reached, skim off and discard the scum, then pot the butter into jars.
